behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources
behaviors.justification: Tests data aggregation at a module boundary. Real failure: source data is dropped, mixed up, or not passed downstream to summary generation. Testing aggregation in isolation (Unit) won't catch if the full dataset isn't handed off correctly. Requires mocking three data sources with distinct test values and verifying the complete aggregated dataset reaches summary_generate().
behaviors.test_description: Mock three distinct data sources (metrics endpoint, issue tracker, deployment log) with known test values, execute data collection and aggregation logic, and assert the aggregated input passed to summary_generate() contains all fields from all three sources
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration level is necessary because the failure is at the boundary between aggregation and summary generation; Unit testing the aggregation function alone wouldn't verify the data is fully passed downstream.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a 'data unavailable' note for that section rather than aborting
behaviors.justification: Tests error handling across module boundaries. Real failure: script crashes instead of continuing, or report generation doesn't include error message. Testing error handling logic in isolation (Unit) won't catch if the report generation step doesn't actually produce the error note. Requires mocking a source failure and verifying the complete flow handles it gracefully.
behaviors.test_description: Mock one data source to raise an exception simulating unavailability, execute the full script, and verify the report is generated successfully with a 'data unavailable' note for that section rather than aborting
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration is correct because the failure mode spans modules (error in collection → report generation behavior); testing error handling alone wouldn't verify the downstream report generation includes the error section.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data
behaviors.justification: The phrase 'narrative summary' indicates natural language generation, strongly suggesting an LLM-based approach. Accuracy of LLM output is non-deterministic: the real model may summarize the same data differently across runs. Mocking or stubbing the LLM would hide whether the real LLM produces an accurate narrative. Real failure: real LLM generates a summary that omits key metrics, misrepresents values, or produces inaccurate representations. Testing requires running the actual LLM to verify its output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Provide test data containing specific measurable key values (e.g., metric counts, issue totals, deployment dates) to the summary generation function, execute with real LLM integration, and verify the generated narrative accurately represents and includes those key values
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Agentic level is necessary because the failure depends on real LLM output variability; Integration testing with a mocked LLM would not catch real accuracy failures.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly
behaviors.justification: The behavior specifically states the report 'reaches the configured recipient's inbox.' Mocking the email service would allow a test to pass while the email never actually reaches any real inbox (MOCK-HIDE violation). Real failure: email doesn't reach inbox due to SMTP connection issues, misconfigured recipient, delivery service failures, or email routing problems. Testing at Integration with mock assertions would verify send() was called, but not that delivery succeeded. Real inbox delivery requires System-level infrastructure (real SMTP server or test email service with actual delivery).
behaviors.test_description: Execute the complete end-to-end pipeline from data collection (all three sources operational) through report generation and email delivery, using real email infrastructure (SMTP server or test email service), and verify the report is successfully delivered to the configured recipient's inbox with correct and complete content
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: System level is required because mocking email would hide the real failure of email not reaching the inbox; actual delivery infrastructure is necessary to verify the behavior.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run
behaviors.justification: Tests error handling and retry logic across modules. Real failure: file not saved after email failure, or retry logic doesn't check for and process persisted failures. Testing retry logic in isolation (Unit) won't catch if the file isn't actually written or read. Testing requires: (1) mocking email to fail, verifying file is saved with correct content; (2) simulating a second script invocation, verifying it detects the persisted failure and retries delivery. This is Integration-level: local filesystem I/O, mocked external service, and module interaction.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the email service to raise an exception on the first invocation (simulating service unavailability), execute the script, and verify the report is saved to the configured local file with correct content. Then execute a simulated second scheduled run with email service available, and verify the script detects the persisted failure file and successfully retries delivery
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration is the minimum level; while System testing of the actual scheduler running twice would be valuable, the core behavior—persistence and retry logic—can be tested at Integration by mocking two sequential invocations.
